544fcfb3-be56-46e7-9ca4-4c6e7bf17e5b.prompt:
Context: play, classic, music, youtube, give, robot, script, play, classic, song, youtube
give the robot script to play classic song in the youtube
544fcfb3-be56-46e7-9ca4-4c6e7bf17e5b.response:
Here is a simple robot script to play a classic song on YouTube:
YouTube Classic Song Player Script
import webbrowser
import pywhatkit

def play_classic_song(song_name):
    # Open YouTube in the default web browser
    url = f"https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query={song_name}"
    webbrowser.open(url)

    # Use pywhatkit to play the song
    pywhatkit.playonyt(song_name)

# Example usage:
play_classic_song("Moonlight Sonata")
To use this script:
Install the required libraries by running pip install pywhatkit in your terminal/command prompt.
Save this script to a file (e.g., classic_song_player.py).
Run the script using Python (e.g., python classic_song_player.py).
Replace "Moonlight Sonata" with the name of your desired classic song.
Note: This script uses the pywhatkit library to play the song on YouTube. If you don't have this library installed, you can install it using pip.

YouTube Classic Song Player .robot Script
*** Settings ***
Library  Process

*** Variables ***
${SONG_NAME}  Moonlight Sonata

*** Test Cases ***
Play Classic Song
  Open Browser  https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=${SONG_NAME}
  Sleep  5 seconds
  Close Browser
To use this script:
Install the Robot Framework by running pip install robotframework in your terminal/command prompt.
Install the SeleniumLibrary by running pip install robotframework-seleniumlibrary in your terminal/command prompt.
Save this script to a file (e.g., classic_song_player.robot).
Run the script using the Robot Framework (e.g., robot classic_song_player.robot).
Replace Moonlight Sonata with the name of your desired classic song.
Note: This script uses the SeleniumLibrary to open a web browser and navigate to the YouTube search results page.
